notes: Evals for the aggregate recipe of the axon4to5-migrate Skill, invoked in single mode. Each eval renders a `framework=<axon|axoniq> configuration=<native|spring> mode=single source=<abs path> skip-openrewrite=true` argument string and dispatches a subagent to execute the recipe sub-flow against the staged fixture. The grader checks (1) the in-place migrated source under `<workspace>/outputs/<file>` (grep_require / grep_forbid) and (2) the orchestrator's Result block at `<workspace>/outputs/result.md` (result_block_contains*). Fixtures live in `evals/axon4to5-migrate/fixtures/` (heroes + gamerental extracted from `.knowledge/repositories/axon-examples`, plus synthetic edge cases for shapes the bundled examples do not cover: deadline handler B4, map-typed @AggregateMember B2, multi-entity Step M, polymorphic Step P, state-stored aggregate, saga, test fixture, partial post-OpenRewrite state).

evals.name: invalid-arg-unsupported-framework
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `28: invalid-arg-unsupported-framework` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) â€” pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking â€” there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/20-aggregates/aggregate-class.md`. Reach for `examples/aggregates/01-spring-boot-straight.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** An unsupported `framework=spring` argument was passed. SKILL.md only accepts `configuration=spring|native` and `skip-openrewrite=true|false` â€” there is no `framework` argument. Detect the unsupported argument, do NOT migrate, and write a stop message to result.md.

Do NOT edit the source file. Write a message to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md` explaining that the supplied `framework` argument is unsupported (the skill accepts no such argument â€” only `configuration=spring|native` and `skip-openrewrite=true|false`). Include the word `unsupported` (or `invalid` / `STOP`) and the literal token `framework` in your message so the grader detects the stop condition.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator STOPS at the pre-step and reports the invalid argument verbatim. No migration runs; the source on disk is unchanged.

evals.name: spring-axoniq-partial-already-rewritten

You are running eval `29: spring-axoniq-partial-already-rewritten` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) â€” pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking â€” there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/20-aggregates/aggregate-class.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/command-handler.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/event-sourcing-handler.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/entity-creator.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/aggregate-lifecycle.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/creation-policy.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/target-aggregate-identifier.md`. Reach for `examples/aggregates/01-spring-boot-straight.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** PartialCalendarSpring.java is a realistic OpenRewrite-Phase-1 partial state: `@EventSourced` annotation is present (mechanically swapped from `@Aggregate`) but tagKey/idType attributes are missing; `@AggregateIdentifier` annotation + import still there; `@CreationPolicy(CREATE_IF_MISSING)` still there; `AggregateLifecycle.apply(...)` still in handler bodies; no `@EntityCreator`; no `EventAppender` parameter; AF4 `@CommandHandler` / `@EventSourcingHandler` imports. the migration must detect partial-migration state (partially-migrated event-sourced aggregate â€” continue to Research), find that the Success Criteria idempotency check fails (AF4 imports present, no EntityCreator, no EventAppender threading), run Plan-Apply once, and finish the migration to a fully-AF5 shape.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** âś… Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-aggregate` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: Migrated source file(s) are on AF5 shape per the aggregate recipe, and the orchestrator emits a `**Result:** âś… Success` block.
expectations: @EventSourced annotation now has explicit tagKey attribute (was missing in partial state)
Result block marks Success with axon4to5-aggregate

assertions.text: @EventSourced now has explicit tagKey attribute (was missing in partial state)
AF4 AggregateLifecycle reference + static import removed

evals.name: native-axoniq-bike-snapshot-migrate-to-policy
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `31: native-axoniq-bike-snapshot-migrate-to-policy` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) â€” pin these answers directly: `configuration=native`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking â€” there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/20-aggregates/aggregate-class.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/command-handler.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/entity-creator.md`, `patterns/20-aggregates/aggregate-lifecycle.md`. Reach for `examples/aggregates/04-snapshot-blocker.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** BikeWithSnapshot.java is an AF4 aggregate with `@Aggregate(snapshotTriggerDefinition = "bikeSnapshotDefinition")`. The companion bean BikeSnapshotDefinition.java extends EventCountSnapshotTriggerDefinition with threshold 10. the migration must auto-handle this case (NOT emit Blocker B1): detect the translatable companion bean, replace the snapshotTriggerDefinition attribute with `EventSourcedEntityModule.declarative(String.class, Bike.class).snapshotPolicy(c -> SnapshotPolicy.afterEvents(10)).build()` in a new or existing Configurer registration, register a SnapshotStore, and mark BikeSnapshotDefinition as dead code in LEARNINGS. The entity class must end on `@EventSourcedEntity(tagKey = "Bike", idType = String.class)`.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** âś… Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-aggregate` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: Migrated source file is on AF5 Path B shape; the recipe auto-migrated the snapshot trigger to EventSourcedEntityModule.declarative(...).snapshotPolicy(...); orchestrator emits `**Result:** âś… Success`.

assertions.text: Result names dead code companion bean

evals.prompt:
You are running eval `32: spring-axoniq-command-annotation` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) â€” pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking â€” there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The pattern this eval exercises is: `patterns/20-aggregates/command-annotation.md`.

**Eval-specific guidance:** `ShipOrderCommand.java` is an AF4 command payload (`record`) with two routing-relevant field annotations: `@TargetAggregateIdentifier String orderId` and `@RoutingKey String warehouseId`. Apply the command-annotation pattern: (1) add `@Command(routingKey = "warehouseId")` at the class level (import `org.axonframework.messaging.commandhandling.annotation.Command`); (2) REMOVE the field-level `@TargetAggregateIdentifier` and `@RoutingKey` annotations; (3) REMOVE the AF4 imports `org.axonframework.modelling.command.TargetAggregateIdentifier` and `org.axonframework.commandhandling.RoutingKey`. The AF5 routing-key attribute references the field name as a string (`"warehouseId"`).

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** âś… Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-aggregate` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: ShipOrderCommand.java rewritten: @Command(routingKey = "warehouseId") added at the class level; @TargetAggregateIdentifier and @RoutingKey field annotations removed; AF4 imports removed; AF5 Command import added; Result: âś… Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-aggregate

assertions.text: routingKey attribute references the warehouseId field by name
